ogTitle: Ferny Hills vs Ficks Crossing: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Ferny Hills and Ficks Crossing.
heading: Ferny Hills vs Ficks Crossing — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ferny Hills, Queensland and Ficks Crossing, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ficks Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Ferny Hills (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ferny Hills or Ficks Crossing safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ferny Hills has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Ficks Crossing scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ferny Hills scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Ficks Crossing scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ferny Hills or Ficks Crossing?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ficks Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
